note:
{
  "experiment_overview": "The experiment involves the synthesis and characterization of semiconductor nanocrystals (NCs) using ZnSe/CdS/Pt as a model system. The goal is to study the optical properties and hydrogen production efficiency of these nanocrystals.",
  "procedures_observed": [
    "A single nanoparticle is shown, consisting of ZnSe, CdS, and Pt. The experiment begins with the preparation of solutions containing ZnAc, CdAc, and NaOH in MeOH.",
    "The solution is heated in a water bath at 95°C for 8 hours.",
    "After heating, the solution is cooled and filtered to remove any undissolved particles.",
    "The resulting solution is then characterized by measuring its absorbance and emission spectra."
  ],

    "ZnAc, CdAc, NaOH, MeOH, water bath, filter",
    "ZnSe/CdS/Pt nanocrystals"
  ],

    "Computer for data analysis and graphing"
  ],

    "Time: 8 hours",
    "Absorbance/Emission measurements taken over time"
  ],

    "Heating solution",
    "Cooling and filtering",
    "Characterization of solution"
  ],

    "Hydrogen production rate over time"
